aliases: Máng guǒ gān jú huǒ shān tián tián quān

romanized: Máng guǒ gān jú huǒ shān tián tián quān

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic doughnut food product class; no specific filled mango-mandarin variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A039V covers doughnuts as a category; no specific filled fruit-cream variant. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for this filled doughnut variant. None assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard volcano donut piece weighing approximately 80 g, as typically served at Kazo Premium and similar Singapore artisan donut outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, glazed, enriched (includes honey buns) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #18249; adapted with fruit-filled cream adjustment using USDA FoodData Central #168917 (Doughnuts, cake-type, plain) and HPB Singapore nutrient composition data for filled pastries
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact match exists for a mango-mandarin filled volcano donut in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA databases. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy yeast-leavened glazed doughnut (#18249, ~380 kcal/100g) as the primary structural base, with upward adjustment for fruit-cream filling (mango, mandarin, cream) increasing sugar_g and fat_g modestly. The adaptation is conservative and consistent with comparable filled artisan doughnuts in the USDA Branded Food database. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Doughnut, white flour, deep-fried — GI value 76 (glucose reference); Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 76 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for a standard white-flour deep-fried doughnut. The Mango Mandarin Volcano Donut is a yeast-leavened, deep-fried, filled doughnut made with enriched wheat flour, which is structurally analogous. The fruit-cream filling adds sugar but does not materially lower the GI relative to the base dough. GL per serving = (76 × 41.6 g carbohydrate per 80 g serving) / 100 = 31.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~224 mg per 80 g serving) and high refined-carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit consumption of this donut to occasional treats, as the sodium content (~224 mg per piece) and high refined-carbohydrate load can contribute to fluid retention and elevated blood pressure when consumed regularly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (76) and high glycemic load (~31.6 per serving) from refined wheat flour and added sugars
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this donut due to its high GI of 76 and glycemic load of approximately 31.6 per piece, which will cause a rapid and substantial postprandial blood glucose spike; if consumed, it should be paired with protein or fibre to blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~6 g per serving), trans fat (~0.16 g per serving), and high sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this donut only occasionally, as its saturated fat (~6 g per piece), small trans fat content, and high added sugar (~17.6 g per piece) collectively contribute to dyslipidaemia risk and endothelial inflammation when eaten frequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Mango Mandarin Volcano Donut is a premium artisan doughnut sold in Singapore, notably by Kazo Premium at Funan Mall. It is a yeast-leavened, deep-fried doughnut made from enriched wheat flour dough, filled with a mango and mandarin orange cream or curd filling that erupts ('volcano-style') when bitten into. The exterior is typically glazed or dusted with sugar, and the filling combines the tropical sweetness of ripe mango with the bright citrus notes of mandarin orange. The texture is soft and pillowy on the outside with a rich, flowing fruit-cream centre. Nutritionally, one piece (~80 g) provides approximately 312 kcal, 14.4 g fat (6 g saturated), 41.6 g carbohydrate, and 17.6 g sugar, making it a high-energy, high-sugar dessert item. Its glycemic index of 76 (high) and glycemic load of ~31.6 per serving make it unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The sodium content (~224 mg per piece) is moderate. This donut is part of Singapore's growing premium artisan donut culture, influenced by Japanese and Western donut trends, and is commonly consumed as a dessert, snack, or café treat. It is vegetarian and typically halal-certified at Kazo Premium outlets.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Mango Mandarin Volcano Donut?
Is the Kazo volcano donut halal?
What is the GI of a filled fruit donut?
Can diabetics eat a mango donut?
How much sugar is in a volcano donut?
What is inside a volcano donut?

confidence_reason: No exact match for a mango-mandarin filled volcano donut exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy yeast-leavened glazed doughnut data with conservative adjustments for fruit-cream filling. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (white-flour deep-fried doughnut, GI 76) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence is appropriate given the adaptation basis.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy doughnut reference entry and is not expected to be a meaningful contributor in this product. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary reference source. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the adapted source. Serving size of 80 g is an estimate based on typical artisan donut weights in Singapore; the actual weight of the Kazo Premium product was not independently verified. Halal status is assumed based on Kazo Premium's general market positioning but should be verified against current MUIS certification. Trans fat value of 0.2 g/100g is a conservative estimate consistent with partially hydrogenated oil use in commercial frying; actual value may be lower if the outlet uses non-hydrogenated oils.
